description_long: This object is a standard urban traffic light with a rectangular yellow housing and three circular signal lights arranged vertically. The topmost lens is illuminated red, while the middle and bottom lenses are dark, indicating inactive yellow and green signals. Each signal light is recessed within a black hood that extends outward to shield the lenses from sunlight and improve visibility. The housing appears to be made of painted metal or durable plastic, with a slightly glossy finish and visible seams along the edges. The rear of the unit is solid and unadorned, likely providing access for maintenance and wiring. The overall design is robust and functional, suitable for mounting on poles or overhead supports at intersections.
description: A yellow rectangular traffic light with three vertically stacked circular lenses, the top one glowing red and the others dark, each recessed under a black hood.
description_view_0: Front view showing all three circular signal lights, with the top lens illuminated red and the others dark.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the protruding black hoods over each signal light and the depth of the housing.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the solid, featureless back panel of the traffic light housing.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the three black hoods and the yellow edge of the housing.
